description_long: This is a vintage monoplane with a distinctive gull-wing design, featuring a fabric-covered frame and prominent black cross insignias on each wing. The wings are high-mounted and exhibit a pronounced dihedral angle, supported by a network of tension wires. The fuselage is slender and tapers toward the tail, which has a conventional tailplane with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the engine, which is a front-mounted, exposed piston engine with a wooden propeller. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable, giving the aircraft a classic early-aviation appearance.
description: A vintage, fabric-covered monoplane with high gull wings, open cockpit, exposed front piston engine, and fixed landing gear with black cross insignias.
description_view_0: Side view showing the high gull-wing profile, exposed engine, and open cockpit behind the propeller.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the full wingspan, black cross insignias, and the network of supporting wires.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the gull-wing shape and showing the tail structure and landing gear.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the curved wings, open cockpit, exposed engine, and fixed landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing monoplane with pronounced gull-wing dihedral and straight leading edges, supported by external bracing wires, no winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted exposed piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the engine.
